item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was undeservedly bashed by some long term members that, in my opinion, fail to see that the fact that something is simple or not excessively original doesn't mean it's bad or boring. This is certainly not a mental exercise or convoluted text that has to be read multiple times nor it aims to be, and that's ok. Also, the idea of a tie wearing another tie made me exhale through my nose. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am somewhat surprised by how much this one was upvoted. I do get the reason, but I still think the execution was very bad.

The core concept is good, and do have potential to add enough to the basic trope of "joke on business" to stand on its own in the main list. On the other side, overwhelming even more the hints to something more serious with jokes can still save this for a -J. As it stands, though, it is none and I found it boring.

My suggestion, if the author ever seeks to rewrite this enough, is to play it more serious, to give more to the objects affected. They aren't just inapt or ignorant, they are incoherent. Their parlance doesn't translate into complex jargon, but it doesn't imply someone trying, either, or something alien using a seemingly understandable language. It is just garbage for the sake of nothing.

So, the problem isn't with how many times they speak the word "business". It is what this repetition tell to the reader. It currently tells nothing. Their ability to create a makeshift conference room and the allusion to a religion are interesting points lost in a sea of insufferable repetition of random words without any purpose at all except saying "business".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aha! A question!

any inanimate object

Now while I do say that the object used must be inanimate, there is the very real possibility that this isn't how it always was. Why do we wear ties? Maybe there are other ties like this, and some work on living things? So my answer is yes, it is possible that these ties were used on humans (or still are), and that it could have been one of the ways in which the alien culture of "business" rubbed off on to humanity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, what I'm getting out of this is— capitalism is a theocratic culture propagated by extra- dimensional interlopers.

Definitely seems like it should be a -J, except for the subtle hints. I like how it's hidden in the whole "Business" thing, although I do believe that the Metal Box Team seems a bit incompetent.

Overall, I like it. +1.

Edit: I have a question, just to sate my own personal curiosity: From what I understand, any object that is capable of "wearing" a tie in any capacity will become an instance of 2749-A? So, would that imply that older Business, Inc. operations on Earth did so by making humans into 2749-A?

I don't know if I'm reading too much into this, but that thought hit my brain and wouldn't leave me alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I better say that the image is my own. >w>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I vote for these changes as well, but have upvoted it as written. I really think going farther with implying "business" is an alien memetic construct would give this more substance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I recommend putting more hints into the article, because that concept is a lot more interesting than what's currently there.

+ ideas...
- OK
  • Instead of saying that Business Incorporated "used to" have a branch in the US, I would say that they've been operating on Earth for a long time (decades at least), and currently have lots of branches all over the world.
  • The dialogue of the conference is good, but kind of clashes with the idea that these things are, in fact, massively successful on our planet. Maybe instead of the success rate going down, have the employees show concern that their rate of expansion is getting slower?
  • Maybe hint that the tie also works on humans, or that Business Incorporated has also made ties that affect humans this way? The disconnect between humans performing these behaviors and inanimate objects doing so is a big one, and hard for the reader to cross without being pushed.
  • I'll bookmark this and come back to it later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I found the repetition of Buisness to be a bit boring, and some of the extradimensional stuff seems unnecessary, but this is a real cute SCP and I overall enjoyed it.

+1

item.title: Do a business!
item.content:encoded: This reminded me too much of Vincent Adultman not to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You've got the dash 1 A3 talking where the Dr. should be in the final interview, second-to-last line.

And I'm wanting to believe this is a much less pushy, productivity focused branch related to the Door-to-door salesman's group.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The more I think about it, the more I like this one.
Fridge +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Their boss is a tie… Wearing a tie.
Overall, it was a little boring but amusing enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bit cheesy at the end, but I liked it overall. +1

Kept thinking of The Lego Movie when I read it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've removed most of the "business", but I've kept the names of the services (fast business etc.), because that's their way of advertising their industry.
Also,

+ Spoilers
- If you haven't already got it

Modern business (annoying phrases, ties, etc.), is actually an alien culture.
The purpose of business inc. is to spread (or deliver) this culture in order to honour their God.

If you are wondering, "Wait, when did they say this?", the laptop in the 2nd interview says that one of their previous locations, the USA must have influenced the foundation (judging by their ties). The god thing, that comes from the Lord tI thing Mr. business is quoted on. Maybe I should make this plot more clear with edits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh. I found this to be amusing enough, though I agree that the constant repetition of the term business is a little annoying by the time the article is finished. Still, the inanimate objects have an argument in a roughly constructed board room, and Mr. Business being a tie wearing yet another tie got a decent chuckle out of me.

I'm going to go ahead an give you the +1 for now, but suggest that maybe this could be re-purposed as a -J if a dash more jokes are added in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found this mildly amusing until the constant repetition of "business" made me think of the Lego Movie. I'm not sure this quite works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes the absence of "synergy", "core", "paradigm", "mission", and so on is conspicuous. This doesn't seem to be more than a one note joke, and the single note isn't even well thought out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The description states that the object is a "black and white cotton tie", but the picture is of a black and red tie. One or the other needs to change.

Also, "circumvate the items anomalous effects". I think you were going for circumvent, as circumvate isn't a word and circumvent has the meaning you wanted from the context. And "items" needs to be items' (with an apostrophe at the end), as it is plural possessive.

Overall, I'm getting a meh feeling, but the fixes might improve it for others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm still not sure I can see much substance here beyond "inanimate objects spout the word 'business' a lot" (it's not even really business jargon) and that isn't enough for me. Business Incorporated seems to have no purpose (or its purpose is to make everything into a -1 instance), which doesn't make for much of a story

In addition this has a number of annoying inconsistencies - the tie is described as black and white, in contrast to the picture, and the effect is described as being negated on removal of the tie, which I originally read as referring to the large tie, meaning multiple instances would be impossible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Humor reliant on corporate buzzwords are at about the same level as Yakov Smirnoff parody jokes to me. It's been done so many times, in so many ways, that I just feel nothing, except maybe slight annoyance, when I see it. That's probably more personal preference than anything, though I can't help but think that maybe there's something more worthwhile to reach for than literally sticking neckties on things and having them jabber about success rates.

In terms of what I view to be objective quality, I do think that beating the joke into the ground repeatedly makes for a pretty tired reading experience. And the interviews. More aggressively wacky things shouting at me in all caps to get across the huge amounts of forced whimsy. Lord preserve us.

item.title: Allow me to bring this back up to +1
item.content:encoded:
While this concept has kinda been done before with Moth Helm and such, I feel this is still pretty well written and entertaining. Having this:

SCP-2749-A3: He says: "The calls of our lord to business are a force that cannot be described in words or even thoughts."

immediately followed by:

SCP-2749-A3: Remember doctor, it's everyone's business!

particularly made me chuckle. +1 (btw, hope you don't mind me taking the liberty to fix some minor spelling/grammar issues)

Edit: still enjoyed this one, but re-reading, I do agree with Conwell down there that this might work better as a -J perhaps.
